title: Guvernare prin abdicare: Cum frica de semnătură a miniștrilor transformă detaliile tehnice în legi și anulează Parlamentul
summary: The article analyzes how Romanian ministers abuse Emergency Ordinances (OUG) to avoid personal legal responsibility, turning trivial administrative details into primary legislation. This 'fear of signature' mechanism dilutes individual ministerial accountability into collective government decisions, effectively bypassing Parliament and creating a vicious cycle of legislative dysfunction.
talking_points: Ministers use Emergency Ordinances instead of ministerial orders to avoid personal legal liability, as OUGs are considered collective political acts protected from criminal prosecution
Trivial technical details like laptop specifications for local administration end up regulated through Emergency Ordinances, creating absurd legislative rigidity
Parliament is reduced to a rubber-stamp institution, flooded with hundreds of OUGs regulating minor issues instead of fulfilling its deliberative and oversight role
The system creates a vicious cycle: existing OUGs can only be modified by new OUGs, trapping any future government in the same pattern
Even ideological opponents like the sovereignist AUR party have valid procedural complaints about the abuse, resorting to boycotting OUG votes in the Senate
author: Redacția
